title: Flaky Fried French Papelon Choux Pastry
description: A weeknight French dish from the Alsace region featuring poached papelon in a flaky choux pastry, with a bitter and glassy-jelly texture, suitable for vegetarians. This dish is a unique blend of French cuisine and vegetarian-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the water, butter, and salt, and bring to a boil.
Add the flour to the saucepan and stir to combine, then remove from heat.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, until the mixture is smooth.
Poach the papelon in boiling water for 10 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Pipe the choux pastry mixture into long strips, about 1 inch wide.
Fry the pastry strips in the hot oil until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Drain the fried pastry on paper towels and fill with the poached papelon.
Sprinkle the tops with sugar and serve immediately.
